concepts.lesson: A tissue is a group of cells that are similar in structure and work together to perform a specific function. In multicellular organisms, tissues help in efficient functioning because specialized cells perform specific tasks.
concepts.real_example: Muscle tissue helps in movement of body parts.

concepts.lesson: Meristematic tissues are plant tissues that actively divide and help in the growth of plants. These tissues are found in growing regions like the tips of roots and stems.
concepts.real_example: Apical meristem helps the root and stem grow longer.
key_points: Types include apical, lateral and intercalary

concepts.lesson: Permanent tissues are formed when meristematic cells lose the ability to divide and take up specific roles. These tissues perform specialized functions in plants.
concepts.real_example: Parenchyma tissue stores food in plants.

concepts.lesson: Simple permanent tissues consist of only one type of cell performing the same function. The main types are parenchyma, collenchyma, and sclerenchyma.
concepts.real_example: Collenchyma provides flexibility to stems and leaves.

concepts.lesson: Complex permanent tissues are made of more than one type of cell working together to perform a common function. Xylem and phloem are examples.
concepts.real_example: Xylem transports water from roots to leaves.
key_points: Made of different types of cells

concepts.lesson: Epithelial tissue forms the protective covering of the body and lines internal organs and cavities. These cells are tightly packed and act as a barrier between the body and the environment.
concepts.real_example: Skin is made of epithelial tissue.

concepts.lesson: Connective tissue connects, supports, and protects different parts of the body. It has cells embedded in a matrix which may be fluid, solid or jelly-like.
concepts.real_example: Bone supports the body and gives structure.

concepts.lesson: Muscular tissue consists of muscle fibers that contract and relax to produce movement. It helps in movement of body parts and internal organs.
concepts.real_example: Skeletal muscles help move arms and legs.

concepts.lesson: Nervous tissue is responsible for receiving stimuli and transmitting nerve impulses throughout the body. It is composed of specialized cells called neurons.
concepts.real_example: The brain sends signals to muscles through neurons.
key_points: Present in brain and spinal cord

concepts.lesson: A neuron is the basic unit of nervous tissue. It consists of a cell body, dendrites and a long axon. These structures help in transmitting nerve impulses from one part of the body to another.
concepts.real_example: Signals from the brain to the hand travel through neurons.
